# Encountered Issues — Recurring Bug Pattern Catalog

> **Auto-generated. Do not hand-edit.** To add a pattern, add a
> `<!-- sharkrite-recurring-pattern -->` block to the closed issue or PR body
> and run `rite --refresh-encountered-issues`.

This catalog lists bug classes that recurred 2+ times during Sharkrite
dogfooding. Its purpose: let future Claude sessions recognize a familiar
pattern quickly and apply the known fix rather than re-diagnosing from scratch.

Each entry includes: the pattern name, recurrence instances (PRs + dates),
root-cause class, and the mitigation or lint rule that prevents recurrence.

## How to Register a Pattern

Add the following block to a **closed** issue or PR body (not as a code example —
the actual HTML comment block), then run `rite --refresh-encountered-issues`:

```
<!-- sharkrite-recurring-pattern -->
**Pattern:** <Short name matching the issue title>
**Root Cause:** <Bug class — e.g. "unanchored grep matches documentation examples">
**Variants:** <Other places in the codebase where this pattern appears>
**Mitigation:** <Fix or lint rule that prevents recurrence>
<!-- /sharkrite-recurring-pattern -->
```

**Format anchor:** The harvester requires the full HTML comment form
(`<!-- sharkrite-recurring-pattern -->`). A body that merely documents the marker
string as inline text (e.g. in a code span or prose) will **not** match — this
prevents documentation examples from being ingested as real pattern entries.

**Transition:** Issues with the legacy `recurring-pattern` label are still ingested
during the transition period so pre-marker patterns are not lost.
